title: chamaesyce celastroides var. laehiensis
intro: Chamaesyce celastroides var. laehiensis, also known as the Hawaiian spurge or the lae'i spurge, is a variety of the Chamaesyce celastroides plant, a type of succulent native to the Hawaiian Islands. The medicinal properties of Chamaesyce celastroides var. laehiensis include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to the presence of various bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and alkaloids. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity, making it a valuable resource for traditional medicine. Chamaesyce celastroides var. laehiensis can be prepared as a tea or a tincture, and it has been traditionally used by indigenous Hawaiians for its medicinal properties.
